features: Mean-pooled CPG node features, zero-padded to 200-dim (0-hop GNN baseline)
training_set: devign_full originals train (21340 samples, pos=9737, neg=11603)
key_findings: Identifier renaming causes the largest F1 drop (-9.1% full / -7.29% 30-trial), suggesting the model relies on identifier-based node features

Dead code insertion anomalously increases Recall (58.5% -> 86.9%) while reducing Precision (53.4% -> 47.6%), likely because added CPG nodes shift the mean-pooled embedding toward patterns correlated with vulnerabilities in the training set
Control flow obfuscation has minimal impact (-1.8%), indicating graph topology features in mean-pooled representation are not strongly affected

GNN training (Devign+DGL, REVEAL+PyTorch) failed to converge on devign_full: DGL 0.4->2.5 API mismatch caused Devign to predict all-positive; REVEAL GNN diverged due to 21340 per-sample gradient steps per epoch causing loss explosion
